summary:
Great! The test now passes. The pagination issue has been fixed. The problem was in the offset calculation for pagination. The original code was incorrectly subtracting 1 from the offset when the page number was greater than 1, which caused page 2 to start at the same index as page 1.

The fix was to use the standard pagination formula:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page
```

This ensures that:
- Page 1 starts at index 0 (offset = (1-1)*10 = 0)
- Page 2 starts at index 10 (offset = (2-1)*10 = 10)
- Page 3 starts at index 20 (offset = (3-1)*10 = 20)
- And so on...

The test confirms that the pagination now works correctly across page boundaries.
